element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic aiming and trigger timing with stationary or slow-moving target
Divided attention between two targets, prioritization of aim
Leading moving targets at distance, timing prediction
Reaction time development, sustained accuracy under pressure
Maintaining consistency and accuracy as stakes increase

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Vs. Duck Hunt arcade - after every second round
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Impulse control and target discrimination under duress

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Vs. Duck Hunt arcade - variable rounds
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Extreme attention division and rapid decision-making

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully hitting all 10 targets in a round (bonus points awarded)
Meeting minimum hit requirement to advance
typical_tension_triggers: Each missed shot (depletes the three-attempt budget)
Arcade mode: life loss on missed targets
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-relief: each round presents escalating difficulty within discrete target cycles; player tension builds during the three-attempt window, releases upon round completion or failure; arcade version amplifies tension through cumulative life loss

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Game mode selection (A/B/C) determines difficulty and target presentation; Game A single-duck mode is accessible entry point
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Shooting the hunting dog in arcade bonus stages reveals Easter egg (dog scolds player); impossible to shoot on console (developer stated as intentional family-friendly design choice)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Game A (single duck) presents minimal challenge; targets move predictably; player has three attempts per appearance to build confidence
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Game B (dual ducks) and Game C (clay pigeons) introduce spatial complexity and prediction; progressive speed increases require sustained accuracy; minimum hit thresholds rise incrementally
difficulty_curve.late_game: Rounds 50+ feature very rapid targets and high minimum hit requirements; Round 99 completion triggers kill screen (Round 0) with erratic/nonexistent targets, ending progress indefinitely
notable_spikes: Transition from Game A to Game B (dual-target mental load)
Mid-game rounds where minimum hit threshold jumps significantly
Vs. Duck Hunt arcade: every second round bonus stage (distraction mechanic)
Vs. Duck Hunt arcade: shift to 12-target rounds with occasional triple targets

notable_level_patterns.description: Each round consists of 10 targets (12 in arcade) presented sequentially; player must hit minimum number to advance; failure triggers game over. Difficulty scales within round sequence, not spatial design.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Game A Round 1-5 (gentle), Game A Round 15+ (challenging)

notable_level_patterns.description: Three core game modes (A: single duck; B: dual ducks; C: clay pigeons) offer different difficulty and spatial challenge profiles without map changes; arcade alternates B and C with bonus stages
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Game A vs Game B vs Game C

notable_level_patterns.description: Arcade-exclusive pattern: after every second round, ducks fly from grass while hunting dog occasionally jumps into line of fire as intentional distraction. Reward for accuracy, penalty for impulsive shooting.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Vs. Duck Hunt arcade - between rounds 2-3, 4-5, 6-7, etc.

notable_level_patterns.description: Reaching Round 99/100 in Game A triggers Round 0 (kill screen) with erratic behavior, impossible targets, and game progression halt; parallels classic arcade kill screen design
